---
title: "Sampling"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(primer.data)
```

```{r}
shaming
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
#| message: false

# 1. Compute the true population mean
truth <- shaming |> 
  summarize(mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE)) |> 
  pull(mean_age)

# 2. Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(10)

# 3. Build a tibble of 1,000 samples
samples <- map_dfr(1:1000, function(id) {
  shaming |> 
    slice_sample(n = 50) |> 
    summarize(
      sample_mean = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
      sample_se = sd(age, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n())
    ) |> 
    mutate(
      replicate = id,
      lower = sample_mean - 1.96 * sample_se,
      upper = sample_mean + 1.96 * sample_se
    )
})
# 4. Filter for the first 100 samples and determine if they cover the truth
plot_data <- samples |> 
  slice_head(n = 100) |> 
  mutate(covers_truth = lower <= truth & upper >= truth)

# 5. Create the publication-ready plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = sample_mean, y = replicate)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper, color = covers_truth), height = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(color = covers_truth), size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = truth, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "darkgreen", "FALSE" = "darkorange")) +
  labs(
    title = "95% Confidence Intervals for 100 Random Samples",
    subtitle = "About 95% of the intervals are expected to catch the true population mean age (red dashed line).",
    x = "Voter Age (Years)",
    y = "Sample Replicate (1 to 100)",
    caption = "Source: shaming dataset from primer.data package",
    color = "Covers Truth"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey35", size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}
# Compute the fraction of the 1,000 intervals that contain the true population mean
coverage_fraction <- samples |> 
  summarize(fraction_covering = mean(lower <= truth & upper >= truth)) |> 
  pull(fraction_covering)

# Print the fraction
coverage_fraction
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
#| message: false

# 1. Compute the true population mean
truth <- shaming |> 
  summarize(mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE)) |> 
  pull(mean_age)

# 2. Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(10)

# 3. Build a tibble of 1,000 biased samples
biased_samples <- map_dfr(1:1000, function(id) {
  shaming |> 
    mutate(sampling_weight = if_else(age > 40, 3, 1)) |> 
    slice_sample(n = 50, weight_by = sampling_weight) |> 
    summarize(
      sample_mean = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
      sample_se = sd(age, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n())
    ) |> 
    mutate(
      replicate = id,
      lower = sample_mean - 1.96 * sample_se,
      upper = sample_mean + 1.96 * sample_se
    )
})
```

```{r}
#| message: false

# 1. Compute the true population mean
truth <- shaming |> 
  summarize(mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE)) |> 
  pull(mean_age)

# 2. Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(10)

# 3. Build a tibble of 1,000 biased samples
biased_samples <- map_dfr(1:1000, function(id) {
  shaming |> 
    mutate(sampling_weight = if_else(age > 40, 3, 1)) |> 
    slice_sample(n = 50, weight_by = sampling_weight) |> 
    summarize(
      sample_mean = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
      sample_se = sd(age, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n())
    ) |> 
    mutate(
      replicate = id,
      lower = sample_mean - 1.96 * sample_se,
      upper = sample_mean + 1.96 * sample_se
    )
})

# 4. Compute the average of the 1,000 biased sample means
avg_biased_mean <- mean(biased_samples$sample_mean)

# 5. Plot the density curve of biased sample means
ggplot(biased_samples, aes(x = sample_mean)) +
  geom_density(fill = "royalblue", alpha = 0.3, color = "royalblue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = truth, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = avg_biased_mean, color = "black", linetype = "solid", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Sample Means from Biased Sampling",
    subtitle = "The average sample mean (black line) systematically overestimates the population truth (red dashed line).",
    x = "Sample Mean Age (Years)",
    y = "Density",
    caption = "Source: shaming dataset from primer.data package"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey35", size = 10)
  )
```
